body_txt: Some people laugh as a sign of mental instability. Others speak in a Creepy Monotone or openly show signs of being violent. Then there's the character who's revealed to be insane and shows almost no signs of being crazy until that point... at least not publicly. A character that feigns sanity quite simply is the kind of person that can maintain their façade in most social situations, but is completely insane on the inside. So naturally when the audience figures this out it can sometimes be a major Plot Twist . Other times the character's craziness could be apparent to the audience from the start, and not the other characters. Either way beware of spoilers. The Trope Namer comes from a book called The Mask of Sanity , which describes how people that aren't stable can blend in with society . Yandere , Uncanny Valley Girl and Cute and Psycho are Sub Tropes , when people may act perfectly normal until that certain trigger is pressed, then they quickly turn into murderous psychopaths. Taking off the mask can be seen as a Villainous Breakdown . Note that this doesn't always refer to characters who are violent. More "benign" examples will have a character who secretly suffers from some kind of debilitating mental disorder, but expresses it inwards rather than outwards. Characters of this specific variation are typically written as more sympathetic or even as outright heroes, albeit generally of the troubled Anti-Hero variety. Compare and contrast with Stepford Smiler (Unstable) and Sanity Slippage (a normally sane character becomes increasingly insane). Often overlaps with Reluctant Psycho . Contrast with Obfuscating Insanity . See also Beneath the Mask and Hyde Plays Jekyll . Sympathetic portrayals might do this to avoid the Neurodivergence Stigma .
